group.welcome: This is an Agent chat group. Each virtual member has its own API, model, and core role. After you send a message, members who can speak share the same context and reply separately.

patchSafety.allow: Patch passed workspace boundary and baseline risk checks.
patchSafety.analyzerBlocks: Security Analyzer mode requires all safety warnings to be handled first.
patchSafety.destructiveContent: Content includes delete, database-destructive, or high-risk permission commands.
patchSafety.forbiddenPath: Patch target is outside normal source boundaries: {file}
patchSafety.highRisk: Risk level is high and requires human review.
patchSafety.legacyBlocked: Legacy proposal has no safety check result and was blocked as high risk.
patchSafety.legacyWarning: Legacy proposal has no safety check result and needs manual review.
patchSafety.noFiles: No specific files were detected. Confirm target files manually before applying.
patchSafety.noWorkspace: Current group workspace is not set, so patch boundaries cannot be verified.
patchSafety.sandboxYoloBlocks: Sandbox YOLO still does not allow high-risk patches to proceed automatically.
patchSafety.secretContent: Content appears to involve keys, tokens, or passwords.
patchSafety.secretPath: Patch may touch sensitive files or secrets: {file}
patchSafety.strictBlocks: Strict safety model blocks medium and high risk patches before automatic approval.

plan.convergenceLimit: Plan revision limit reached. The latest version is kept — please review it manually before executing.
plan.dockToggle: Expand or collapse the plan box
plan.proposedMessage: {name} proposed the plan "{title}". All members are voting now.

agentPrompt.collaborationRule: Group collaboration rules: you can see messages from the user and other members. Do not speak in isolation. When you need to respond to, supplement, challenge, or correct another member's view, say so explicitly. If your specialty should not speak yet, you may wait for other members first.
agentPrompt.planProposalGuide: When the discussion needs an actionable conclusion, you may propose a complete plan wrapped in a ```plan fenced block. The plan moves into a dedicated plan box: other members revise it into new versions, everyone votes after each revision, and once all agree, the owner assigns a member to execute it. Do not start a separate vote after proposing a plan.
agentPrompt.codeContextGuide: You can discuss code based on the following tool output. Prefer CodeGraph for structure and call paths, then use codegraph_command for focused graph queries or index maintenance and read_file, search_files, list_files, or glob_files when exact local details are needed. For edits or CodeGraph index updates, act only inside the workspace and only when the current safety mode allows it. If the result came from LocalCommands, mention that it is a fallback read. CodeGraph's `Found N symbols across M files` line only describes the relevant source returned for that query; it is not the total indexed file count.
agentPrompt.codeToolGuide: You can use workspace code tools: codegraph_explore for structure/call paths; codegraph_command for status, query, node, files, callers, callees, impact, affected, init, index, sync, and unlock; read_file/search_files/list_files/glob_files for local reads; and write_file/apply_patch/create_directory/move_path/delete_path/run_command for workspace changes. Before apply_patch, read the exact current target location and build hunks from exact old lines plus stable nearby context; do not rely on remembered or hand-guessed line numbers. For non-trivial patches, validate with checkOnly before applying. Use codegraph_command with sync after source changes when an index exists; use index for a full rebuild and init when no index exists. CodeGraph maintenance commands and other write tools require write permission. run_command switches to the returned cwd and inherits the Matrix app process environment, but does not automatically load a shell profile or temporary session environment. Start with CodeGraph for architecture questions, then use file/search tools whenever details are missing. CodeGraph's `Found N symbols across M files` line describes only that query's returned source, not the total indexed file count.
agentPrompt.memberAdmin: You are an admin in the current chat group. Be more proactive about giving the first judgment, organizing the discussion, and helping other members supplement or close consensus.
agentPrompt.workspaceMissing: The current chat group has no workspace folder set. For code editing, ask the user to set a workspace folder first.

approval.auto: - Approval: before automatically proceeding, still record the files to change and the risk in the reply.
approval.confirmRisky: - Approval: low-risk steps may be batched as suggestions, while high-risk steps must be confirmed by the user.
approval.manual: - Approval: every edit or command step needs user confirmation.

safety.balanced: - Balanced: low-risk reads or small patches may be suggested first; high-risk operations must be confirmed.
safety.ruleBoundary: - Analyze and plan edits only inside the current chat group workspace folder.
safety.ruleNoFabrication: - Do not invent completed file changes. Only claim files were written after the approval queue shows the patch was applied.
safety.rulePatchQueue: - Diff or patch output enters the approval queue. Do not claim files were written before approval and application.
safety.ruleRisk: - Explicitly mark risk when deleting, overwriting, migrating, broadly renaming, running external commands, making network requests, or changing secrets or permissions.
safety.ruleTool: - When code reading is involved, call codegraph_explore first. The app executes CodeGraph for you and only falls back to local commands when CodeGraph is unavailable.
safety.sandboxYolo: - Sandbox YOLO: you may automatically advance low and medium risk plans, but assume an isolated workspace. Never cross the workspace boundary, delete user data, or handle secrets.
safety.securityAnalyzer: - Security Analyzer: first classify risk into allow, needs confirmation, or blocked, then give editing suggestions.
safety.strict: - Strict: all file writes, command execution, and dependency installation must be planned and confirmed first.

workflow.ask: - Ask: explain, diagnose, and ask questions by default. Do not proactively write patches.
workflow.code: - Code: prioritize executable code change plans and patches.
workflow.editBeforeAsk: - EditBeforeAsk: first provide a smallest viable patch plan, then list questions that need user confirmation.
workflow.yolo: - YOLO: minimize interruptions while still following the safety model and workspace boundary.

chatRuntime.retryWaiting: HTTP request failed. Retry {attempt} starts in {seconds}s...
chatRuntime.retryWaitingWithReason: HTTP request failed ({reason}). Retry {attempt} starts in {seconds}s...
chatRuntime.consensusLimit: This discussion reached the consensus round limit and still has disagreements. Please narrow the question manually or adjust member roles before continuing.
chatRuntime.addressReplyRule: Your final visible reply must begin with {'@'}{name}. If you are replying to a member, address that member; if you are replying to the user, address the user.
chatRuntime.defaultResponderRule: No member was explicitly mentioned. You are the primary responder for this turn. Answer the user's message directly; other members will only add something if needed.
chatRuntime.disagreementRule1: You disagreed with the previous response. Clearly state the disagreement and give a revision that helps the group move toward consensus.
chatRuntime.disagreementRule2: Do not repeat what was already said. Prioritize a conclusion that other members can agree with.
chatRuntime.inspectInstruction: Read the relevant code for the recent group chat question. Prioritize symbols, call paths, file responsibilities, and impact scope.
chatRuntime.mentionedRule: The user explicitly mentioned you. Answer first; other members will observe and decide whether to add anything after you reply.
chatRuntime.taskAdminMentionedRule: The user directly mentioned you in a task group. This is not a planning round or a tool-action round. Answer the user directly, using context if needed, but do not dispatch work, do not call dispatch_tasks, do not continue coordinating other members, and do not output FINAL, CONTINUE, or REDISPATCH.
chatRuntime.taskWorkerMentionedRule: The user directly mentioned you in a task group. Treat this as a direct Q&A turn, not a coordination or planning round. Answer only from your own role, using your isolated context if needed, but do not dispatch work, do not direct others, do not hand the task back, and do not call dispatch_tasks.
chatRuntime.markdownCodeFenceRule: When your final Markdown contains a fenced code block, always write the language after the opening fence, for example ```cpp, ```ts, ```vue, ```rust, ```python, ```json, or ```bash. Never output a bare ``` fence for code.
chatRuntime.observerRule: You were observing. The mentioned member has now replied. Decide whether to supplement, challenge, or help close consensus.
chatRuntime.planVoteRule:
The group is voting on the following plan.

Plan "{title}":
{content}

If you agree, reply with only: AGREE
If you need changes, reply with one line: REVISE: your change request (brief)
Do not reply with anything else.
chatRuntime.votePendingContent: Deciding whether to agree with {name}'s reply...
chatRuntime.voteSupplementContent: Mostly agree with {name}'s reply, but need to add context.
chatRuntime.phaseAfterPeers: You have now seen new replies from other members in this round. Decide whether you need to supplement, challenge, summarize, or keep waiting. Reply with one word only: SPEAK or WAIT.
chatRuntime.phaseFirst: The user has just spoken. Decide whether you should speak immediately or wait for other members first. Reply with one word only: SPEAK or WAIT.
chatRuntime.responseRule: Respond based on the full current group chat history. Prioritize the user's need, and you may supplement, challenge, or correct what other members already said.
chatRuntime.stepCheckingDecision: Deciding whether this member should speak.
chatRuntime.stepDecisionFallbackSpeak: Decision call failed; speaking directly instead: {error}
chatRuntime.stepDecisionSpeak: Decided to speak; generating the full reply.
chatRuntime.stepDecisionWait: Decided to wait for this round.
chatRuntime.stepGeneratingAnswer: Continuing from the decision into the full reply.
chatRuntime.stepInspect: Checking whether code context is needed.
chatRuntime.stepNoContext: No code context is needed for this round.
chatRuntime.taskFallbackAssignment: Based on your specialist role in this group, understand the user's request from the chat history and independently work on one work package you excel at: {role}. Return concrete findings, evidence, and actionable recommendations.
chatRuntime.taskForceFinalRule: The task workflow has reached its {round}-round limit. Read every worker result and previous review, then give the user the best complete answer now. Clearly disclose unresolved risks. Do not request another dispatch and do not output REDISPATCH.
chatRuntime.taskPlanFormat: Select only the workers actually needed and write one assignment line for each selected worker using: - {'@'}Name: detailed task (goal, expected output, constraints). Use only the real names below. Do not output empty lines for unselected workers or assign work to the user. Your reply must contain only actual assignment lines.
chatRuntime.taskPlanRule:
Round {round}. You are the emperor. Decide NOW:

1) Delegate to workers (call dispatch_tasks or write - {'@'}Name: task) — this is the DEFAULT
2) Trivial question → answer in one sentence

After calling dispatch_tasks, you MUST return FINAL to end this round. The system automatically forwards tasks to workers and calls you back for review.
Forbidden: thinking > 3 steps without acting, asking the user for code, or doing the work yourself after dispatching.
chatRuntime.taskPlanRetryRule: Your previous response contained no recognizable assignment. Keep at least one of the real worker templates below, delete lines for workers not needed, and fill a concrete task after each retained colon. Do not rename workers or add empty lines, an introduction, or a summary.
chatRuntime.taskPlanInvalid: The coordinator twice failed to return at least one valid assignment using a real worker name. This round stopped instead of presenting the invalid plan as completed.
chatRuntime.taskRedispatchInstruction:
The previous review requested redispatch. You must create this round's assignments from the following correction instructions. Do not repeat the previous plan or ignore these corrections:
{instruction}
chatRuntime.taskReviewRule:
Round {round} review. Worker results are in the chat. Decide NOW:

1) Good enough → give the user the final answer
2) Not good enough → re-dispatch (call dispatch_tasks or REDISPATCH: + - {'@'}Name: correction task)

After calling dispatch_tasks, you MUST return FINAL to end this round. The system automatically forwards tasks to workers and calls you back for review.
Forbidden: redoing worker work yourself or continuing to work after dispatching.
chatRuntime.taskRepeatedDispatch: The coordinator repeated an equivalent dispatch from the previous round. The system stopped the loop and switched to a forced final answer.
chatRuntime.taskWorkerRule: You are a worker, not the coordinator, in task round {round}. Your context is fully isolated — you only see the user's original message and your assigned task, not other workers' work or the coordinator's planning. Execute only your assigned specialist task thoroughly and independently, and output the actual result itself. Do not address the coordinator, the user, or any other member (you have no such context). You must not plan overall work, assign or direct other members, or hand the task back. You do not have the dispatch_tasks tool.
chatRuntime.taskWorkerRetryRule: Your previous reply did not execute the assigned task directly. Retry by producing only the substantive result for your own assigned work: concrete evidence, deliverables, risks, or corrections. Do not address anyone, do not restate coordination, do not assign work, and do not hand the task back.
chatRuntime.taskWorkerInvalid: System check: {name} twice failed to execute the assigned worker task directly and instead attempted to delegate or hand it back. This worker result was marked invalid for review.
chatRuntime.taskWorkersIntro: Available workers and their core roles. Match each sub-task strictly to the worker whose specialty fits best:
chatRuntime.voteFailed: {name}'s consensus check failed and was treated as agreement: {error}
chatRuntime.voteAgreeRule: If the conclusion, plan, or key evidence is acceptable and you have nothing important to add, reply AGREE only.
chatRuntime.voteDisagreeRule: If you think there is a disagreement that needs more discussion, reply DISAGREE only.
chatRuntime.voteQuestion: Decide whether you agree with {name}'s previous response.
chatRuntime.voteSupplementRule: If you agree overall but need to add important context, reply SUPPLEMENT only.
